coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 only (140 bpm ceiling). You're 64 days out and in taper week 21—volume is down 43% from last week (17.2 vs 30.2), which is correct. Your last three runs averaged 124–140 bpm; today stays sub-140. The Julian Alps demands your legs fresh and your aerobic base intact, not depleted. Keep it conversational; if you're at the airport or between meetings, 8 miles is enough. Skip any tempo or vert work this week.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You logged 8h 58m sleep last night at 95% efficiency—excellent. That fuels a light recovery day. Eat to performance: aim for 1.5–2g carbs per kg bodyweight today spread across three meals, with protein at each (25–35g). Post-run, take in 30–40g carbs + 15–20g protein within 30 minutes. Given constant travel for work, prioritize whole foods (rice, pasta, lean meat) over gels and bars; your gut needs to trust what's on race day in Slovenia.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery score is 84/100—you're recovered. Sleep efficiency at 95% is elite. Resting HR stable at 62 bpm (30-day avg 61.5) shows no fatigue accumulation. Hold this: 8+ hours nightly through the taper (only 9 weeks left), hydrate aggressively between flights, and use compression or easy walking on travel days. No HRV data is fine for the phase; focus on the sleep and RHR trend, both green.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're in the mental home stretch. Taper anxiety is normal—the temptation to 'make up' for reduced volume is real and wrong. Trust the block: you've logged the vert, the mileage, the aerobic work in weeks 16–20. The next nine weeks are about arrival, not panic. Review your race strategy (aid station nutrition, pacing in the first 15K, descent technique on the rocky sections), visualize one section per day, and keep runs easy. Your fitness is locked in.
